title: indulgent canadian rice pilaf
description: a salty and satisfying rice pilaf with a satisfying crunch from the sashimi-cut vegetables. this dish is perfect for a light lunch or dinner option.

ingredients.name: unsalted chicken broth
ingredients.notes: make sure the broth is at room temperature before using.

ingredients.name: vegetable oil
ingredients.notes: use a neutral-tasting oil with a high smoke point.

ingredients.name: sashimi-cut carrots
ingredients.notes: look for carrots with a high water content for the best crunch.

ingredients.name: sashimi-cut bell peppers
ingredients.notes: any color bell pepper will work, but red is the most festive.

ingredients.name: sashimi-cut snap peas
ingredients.notes: use fresh snap peas for the best flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: unsalted soy sauce
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality soy sauce for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: add the parsley at the end for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: grated cheddar cheese
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality cheddar cheese for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: toasted sesame seeds
ingredients.notes: add the sesame seeds at the end for the best texture.

instructions: heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
add the sashimi-cut carrots, bell peppers, and snap peas to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are tender and lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes.
add the jasmine rice to the skillet and stir to combine with the vegetables.
add the unsalted chicken broth, soy sauce, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and bring to a boil.
reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender, about 18-20 minutes.
fluff the rice pilaf with a fork and stir in the chopped fresh parsley and grated cheddar cheese.
transfer the rice pilaf to a serving dish and sprinkle with toasted sesame seeds.
serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
